title: Latent stint pace-state features
summary: Recent F1 degradation research models lap times with latent tyre pace and pit-stop state resets, including compound-specific and time-varying degradation.
rationale: Recent F1 degradation research models lap times with latent tyre pace and pit-stop state resets, including compound-specific and time-varying degradation. Prior work tried one simple rolling pace delta, but not a fuller set of reset-aware state summaries.
implementation_hint: Sort by Race, Driver, Stint, LapNumber. Add shift-safe expanding/rolling features within each stint: rolling median lap time, rolling slope of LapTime and LapTime_Delta over 3/5/8 laps, current residual versus stint expanding median, residual z-score within Race+LapNumber field, degradation acceleration, and flags for abrupt pace cliff. Restore original row order before returning.
expected_effect: May capture the latent degradation/pace cliff that triggers pit decisions, improving separability near marginal pit-window laps.
risk: If test rows are not complete chronological sequences, shift features may be partially missing. Rolling features can leak if current/future target-derived columns are used; only observed current and past lap features should be used.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
